key_insight: Atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potentials can reproduce screw-dislocation behavior in bcc refractory metals far better than prior classical and some ML potentials, but accuracy depends strongly on database coverage and element-specific energetics.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable atomistic prediction of flow stress, glide plane, and kink-pair energetics in refractory metals, which are critical for designing high-temperature structural materials.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for group 5 and 6 refractory metals (V, Nb, Ta, Mo, W).
Trained an array of ACE interatomic potentials to target screw-dislocation properties.
Validated dislocation glide, Peierls barriers, and kink-pair activation enthalpies against DFT and continuum models.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W and substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, including reliable kink-pair activation enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient as a validation metric, so dislocation-transferability must be checked with glide-plane and kink energetics, not just barrier shape.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
